# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

```php
<?php namespace App\Services; 
    
class SmsService {   
   private $twilio_accountSid,  $twilio_authToken ,$twilio_phoneNumber ;      //Twilio account sid and auth token for your Twillio Account. Replace them with real data if you have not yet created a twilliono account on their portal
  private $from = 'whatsapp:+14155238886';    # This is the whatsApp number   replace this as per need of your client's phone.
        //You can use other format like "tel:" or you may choose Twilio sandbox for testing, then send messages to SandBox numbers and remove them when in production mode  e.g., 'whatsapp:+14159273860' (in development)
         //For Production use your twillio number or the phone_number you have set up on Twilio portal for SMS service, replace with actual data when ready in production mode.  e.g., 'whatsapp:+1527398640' (in development)
    //Replace these placeholders by your twilliot accountSID and auth Token after creating a Twilio Account on their portal, you can get it from the general settings section of that. Also provide phone numbers as per need for client in all cases production mode , testMode respectively e.g., 'whatsapp:+1486592730', or use SandBox number
